text: Hey Brett — Whitfield's pushing the sound dive to Thursday, front's clearing Wednesday and swells look way cleaner after. Need two AL80s topped off by 1pm if you can swing it.
Thursday's doable but tight, got a cert class that morning burning most the stack. I'll pull 'em for you though, no sweat.
Appreciate it. Also — Karen wants to run that underwater housing again, the one we used for the seagrass survey back in April. That still at the shop or did somebody take it out?
Still here. Fair warning tho — checked it last week and the port O-ring looks a little rough. Want me to swap it before Thursday?
Please. Last thing I need is flooding Whitfield's rig in Wrightsville Sound, she'd never let me hear the end of it 😅
Ha, yeah that tracks. Checked the tide tables btw — current's gonna be hauling Thursday afternoon. Y'all planning to drop around 1030 to catch slack?
That's the plan, slack's around 1045 at the site. Should be an easy drift back to the float as long as Karen doesn't stop to measure every blade of seagrass she sees.
Lol no promises on that. I'll have the tanks and housing ready by 1. Text me when y'all surface.
pulling tanks for the Whitfield dive tomorrow and the secondary reg on the spare is venting slow. you got a backup DIN at the shop or am i driving down tonight
yeah there's a spare yoke-to-DIN adapter in the gray bin under the BCD rack. might be the LP seat — how slow we talking
like a steady trickle, not a blow. probably the seat yeah. i'll swap it out if you got a rebuild kit handy
check the red tackle box on the second shelf, should have an Apeks seat kit in there. what's Whitfield working on out there anyway
seagrass density transects, same stretch near Masonboro. visibility's been soft but she says data beats pretty water 😄
ha fair enough. tide's dropping around 0730 so if y'all push past nine the current's gonna make that stretch a real workout
yeah saw that on the tables, aiming for 0630 splash. gotta be back for an 11 o'clock class at Oakridge so it lines up tight
solid. holler if the reg's cooked and we'll just pull one off the rental rack before you head out

Whitfield had us marking a new seagrass bed north of the usual transects in the Sound today. density on that thing was wild. might be worth bringing your housing out there for the return dive she's already talking about.
wait seriously? how far north we talkin
past the ferry channel markers. viz was rough, maybe 4-5 ft, but the bed itself was dense as a carpet. she logged it fast, already scheming a follow-up for next week 🌊
if you can get me on that boat i'll have the rx100 ready. been wanting to test the new port in low viz conditions anyway
tide drops around 0630 next thursday, whitfield usually wants us in by 0700. might be able to swing a +1 if you're actually serious about the early call
i'm serious. 0630 means i'm at wrightsville ramp by 0615, no excuses, coffee in hand
alright i'll float it to her. it's a research dive not a shoot so you'd be documenting not directing — just want that clear before i ask 🤙
understood completely. you lead, i follow. just don't let me miss this one man
Tommy, Surfline's showing a legit 3-4ft swell window Wednesday morning. That longboard repair done? Need to know before I plan around my lab session out at Wrightsville Sound.
Glassed her up yesterday, sitting out back curing now. That ding went deeper than it looked but she's solid.
Good to hear. Got a PADI open water class all day Tuesday so no shot before then. Can I grab her Wednesday around 6am?
Six is early even for me man lol. Make it 7 and I'll have the coffee going.
7 works. Prof. Whitfield's got us in the Sound by 10 so I'd still catch a couple hours at the beach before I gotta pull gear.
Tide drops around 7:45, should be glassy before the onshore kicks. Decent little window you got there.
Alright that settles it. What do I owe you for the repair?
Sixty flat. Nothin fancy, just solid. She'll handle it.
